content.context: This was the original specialist creation process designed for the Managers layer (Engineer, Designer, PM, Marketing). In that model, Managers were persistent agents with frontmatter files, and specialists were created as .md files in project directories. The architecture has since simplified to Leo directly delegating to ephemeral Sonnet-powered specialists without a Managers intermediary.
content.decision: The original hiring loop (Manager identifies gap → Leo hires specialist → Manager executes) has been replaced by the simpler Leo ↔ Specialists model where specialists are ephemeral sub-agents, not persistent agent files.

original_process: Two triggers: (1) Assemble initial team, (2) Fill specific domain gap
Manager reports to Leo: proposed name, scope, what for, worst case without it
Leo checks for duplicates across managers and projects
Leo formats proposal for owner R2: name, scope, playbook
If approved: Leo creates .claude/specialists/{domain}/{name}.md + KB reference doc
historical_record.original_rule: Recognizing a gap and filling a gap are separate responsibilities. Manager identifies the need, reports to Leo. Leo hires the specialist with cross-cutting view. Manager executes with the new hire.
historical_record.original_why: Leo sees duplication across managers, cross-project reuse across repos, and enforces structural standards. Without Leo's cross-cutting view, managers generate duplicate and inconsistent specialists.

impact: No more specialist .md files to maintain
No more Managers layer between Leo and execution
Token economy preserved: Leo (Opus) thinks, Specialists (Sonnet) execute
content.replaced_by: Leo ↔ Specialists model: Leo orchestrates directly, specialists are ephemeral sub-agents spun up for specific tasks and discarded after. No persistent specialist files, no Managers layer.
